Sequence of Returns Simulator

Monte Carlo analysis of returns, inflation, and dynamic withdrawals.

Investment Horizon

000s
Advanced simulation settings
%

Averages a band of runs around each percentile (P10–P60) to reduce noise. 0 = a single run.

Adds year-to-year persistence by drawing runs of consecutive years averaging this length; set to 1 for fully independent years.

Withdrawal Strategy

000s

Spending Over Time

Bias spending earlier in retirement. The annual real change scales the whole withdrawal each year (negative = spend more now, less later); the early-years bonus adds a flat amount for the first few years (the "go-go years").

%
000s

Paste from a spreadsheet — new lines, tabs, commas, semicolons, or pipes. Negative values are deposits. Last value is reused for remaining years.

Dynamic Adjustments & Guardrails

Dynamic Withdrawal Adjustment

Flexibly adjust your withdrawal amount based on the nominal market return. Balance overrides will ignore the market trigger if your portfolio crosses them.

Low Return
%
000s
000s
Expected
%
000s
000s
High Return
%
000s
000s

Balance-Based Spending Scale

Smoothly scale your total withdrawal based on portfolio balance: below the floor, spending ramps down toward the max cut (reached at $0); above the ceiling, it ramps up by the boost rate for every extra multiple of the ceiling — with no upper cap. Between the two, spending is unchanged. Set a minimum withdrawal to always stress-test depletion, even when the scale would cut spending.

0 = no floor

000s
%

0 = no ceiling

000s
%

Always withdraw at least this amount each year (capped by portfolio balance), even if adjustments or your schedule would go lower.

Portfolio Balance Timeline

Shows the trajectory of the 60th, 50th, 40th, 30th, 20th, and 10th percentile paths.

Explore specific paths

A 3D plot of representative paths from the 10th to 60th percentiles. Column height represents portfolio balance, color reflects the annual market return for that year (Red = crash, Green = boom). Drag to rotate, scroll to zoom, right-drag to pan. Single-click a column to pin that simulation and mouse over each year for values; click the column again or empty space to release. Double-click a column to explore ~200 nearby simulations; double-click again to return.

Dynamic Withdrawals Timeline

Shows how withdrawals adjusted dynamically over time across the tracked percentiles.
